steps.title: Recognize the series with internal punctuation
steps.content: The sentence names three parallel purposes (all gerund phrases): growing diverse plant species, fostering scientific research, and educating the public. The phrase “both native and nonnative” is a clarification that must be set off by commas around “species”—creating a comma within the first item.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule for complex series
steps.content: When items in a series contain internal commas, semicolons (not commas) separate the items to prevent confusion between internal punctuation and item separators. Since the first gerund now reads “growing diverse plant species, both native and nonnative” (with an internal comma), the separator between items must be a semicolon. The structure is: [gerund 1, with internal comma]; [gerund 2]; and [gerund 3]. Choices A and D use comma separation despite the internal punctuation; choice C misplaces the semicolon before rather than after the clarifying phrase. That matches choice B — B is correct.
